title: lewisia howellii
intro: Lewisia howellii, a member of the Saxifragaceae family, is a perennial alpine plant native to the western United States. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it effective in treating wounds and skin irritations. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, such as sore throats, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its expectorant and antiseptic properties. Traditionally, Lewisia howellii is prepared as a tea, tincture, or poultice to harness its health benefits, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety as a medicinal plant.
